# fix(dataflow): close BP-049 classified-data leaks + bump inter-package pins

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `64167f7c1477` — fix(dataflow): close BP-049 classified-data leaks + bump inter-package pins

**Body**:

Three DataFlow surfaces leaked classified field values or field names
(cross-SDK of kailash-rs v3.19.0 BP-049 — issue #520):

M1 — NotFound / trust-audit paths
  express.{read,update,delete} emitted raw PK values into
  _trust_record_failure / _trust_record_success query_params and the
  "express.record_not_found_for_read" log event. Added _safe_record_id
  and _safe_query_params helpers on DataFlowExpress that route PKs
  through format_record_id_for_event; every single-record audit /
  log site now hashes classified PKs before emission.

M2 — Cache key construction
  CacheKeyGenerator now accepts an optional classification_policy and
  hashes classified PK values in params BEFORE the JSON serialisation
  that feeds the MD5 digest. Bumped keyspace version default v1 -> v2
  for cross-SDK parity (pre-fix v1 entries are orphaned on upgrade).
  Plumbed policy through both construction sites: DataFlowExpress and
  DataFlowEngine cache integration.

M3 — Validation error messages
  New dataflow.classification.validation_error.sanitize_validation_error
  helper replaces classified field names with "<classified>" and
  classified values with type-descriptor strings
  ("<classified string>", "<classified int>", etc.). Added
  ValidationResult.classified_field_count aggregate for operator
  alerting. validate_model accepts policy + model_name and routes
  errors through the sanitiser.

Tier 2 integration tests added — test_bp_049_classification_leaks_wiring
covers all three surfaces against real PostgreSQL (16 tests pass).
All pre-existing BP-048 tests still pass (35 total across
test_event_payload_classification + test_classification_mutation_return
+ new BP-049 file).

Also bumped inter-package version pins across 6 pyproject.toml files to
the live round-8 PyPI baselines (kailash 2.8.7, kailash-dataflow 2.0.10,
kailash-nexus 2.1.0, kailash-kaizen 2.7.5, kailash-ml 0.10.0,
kailash-mcp 0.2.5, kailash-pact 0.8.1, kailash-align 0.3.1,
kaizen-agents 0.9.2). Every target version verified live on PyPI before
pinning per rules/deployment.md.

Cross-SDK: this is the Python equivalent of kailash-rs v3.19.0 BP-049.
format_record_id_for_event hash format (sha256:XXXXXXXX 8-hex-prefix) is
intentionally identical across SDKs for forensic correlation.

Closes #520
Closes #516 (triaged as out-of-scope; existing rules cover both asks)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
